channel.title: superluminal-scientist
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Parts List
item.description: This is an internal document that I use to keep track of the parts I use during the development of DigiUma, mostly because a lot of my tools have been lost to time and I need to keep track of purchasing them all again. This should not be trusted as a guide.Copy-paste Icons: ✓ ✗ ESP32-S3 Dev Board ✗ x4 MG90s Micro Servos (specifically the MG version to prevent gear stripping) ✗ a lot of hot glue and patience ✗ 80W 110V Soldering Iron ✗ A beautiful set of Helping Hands with a magnifying glass ✗ 63-37 High Purity In Lead Rosin Core Solder ✗ Flux. Lots of flux. ✗

item.title: About
item.description: Welcome, dear Guinea Pigs! This is my Obsidian Vault where I keep track of my various projects at the insistence of my peers.Here you will find information, internal documentation, logs, and general ramblings about my various experiments and projects as I create them.How often this will update, I don't actually know! I am not usually one to document my progress or projects like this, I typically prefer to keep mental track of what I do, but obviously that has its flaws.Either way, I hope you all enjoy.

item.title: DigiUma - The Framework for Umamusume Prosthetics.
item.description: A project aiming to develop upon the concept of biologically controlled animal prosthetics, focusing on Umamusume/Horse Girls.Over my time in the Umamusume fandom, I have seen something unique in myself and many other people of the community. There exists a rampant desire to become one of these fictional horse girls, sometimes on the same level as a transgender person may desire to become their desired gender, so much so that it spawned its own subgenre of the community. As inexplicable as it is, it is hard to deny that it exists. After spending some time in those specific spaces, I was inspired to create this project after being reminded of the Necomimi Brainwave Cat Ears. This project aims to develop on the concept.The scope of DigiUma is relatively small. I intend to create a set of fully articulated Ears and an articulated Tail, driven by a bespoke Behaviour Engine running on an ESP32-S3. The entire project aims to be as cheap as possible, provided you have the tools at hand already.Eventually, I would love to continue past that point into exoskeleton-based enhancements, but that will have to wait.The progress of this project will be marked in log form, and I will be creating them as I develop upon the project. You can find them starting here: TBW.
item.guid: Projects/Hardware/DigiUma/DigiUma - The Framework for Umamusume Prosthetics..md

item.title: Steamworks4Java - Bringing Ease to Multiplayer Hosting in Java Edition.
item.description: A project aiming to smooth over all of the bumps and rough edges that come with attempting to play Multiplayer in Java Edition.TBW.
